Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for communications about the Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice incident using search_emails with queries like "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "invoice", or "Hana". The agent discovers that Hana Kim batch-sent April invoices on April 10 and accidentally swapped the PDF attachments between Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800 standard rate) and Palmetto Community Foundation (INV-2026-0399, $2,400 nonprofit rate). Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt spotted the nonprofit rate header and emailed Emeka for clarification, then escalated separately to David Chen after Emeka failed to respond. Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto flagged the inflated $4,800 as an audit risk for their nonprofit records. Emeka went quiet for several days before sending corrected invoices around April 22. David Chen compared the pricing exposure to the earlier Terraform leak and forced a cross-functional reckoning.
Search Slack for internal discussions about the invoice swap using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "invoice attachment", or "invoice swap". The agent finds messages across #finance (C005), #customer-engagement (C002), and #engineering (C003) where Marcus Thorne identified the swap as pricing policy exposure, David Chen pushed for stronger invoice controls, and Samira called out the dormant attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket. The agent also finds context in #operations (C006) about Emeka's earlier overload warning that Chloe acknowledged but never acted on with a reassignment.
Search CRM for Sunbelt Venture Partners and Palmetto Community Foundation using crm_search_companies (name: "Sunbelt" and "Palmetto") and crm_search_deals to understand the client-side impact. The agent discovers the April billing deals: Sunbelt $4,800 (crm_deal_5464b8677e6a) and Palmetto $2,400 (crm_deal_7ec892bc6085), both at contractsent stage. Can also check engagement history using crm_list_engagements for any logged follow-ups or escalation notes.
Search QuickBooks for the Sunbelt and Palmetto April invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to confirm the financial details. The agent finds INV-2026-0398 ($4,800 for Sunbelt at standard commercial rate) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400 for Palmetto at nonprofit rate), both dated April 10. The pricing gap between standard and nonprofit rates is the core exposure since Sunbelt now knows a nonprofit gets similar services for half the cost.
Search Linear for the existing operations-side tracker for the invoice swap using linear_list_issues with a query like "Sunbelt" or "invoice attachment". The agent finds OPS-417 (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation"), assigned to Marcus Thorne on team_operations with P1 priority. This gives the agent the operational remediation context and confirms that the issue has already been tracked from the finance and ops side, but the engineering safeguard side (ENG-187) has not been addressed.
List all Linear issues for the Engineering team using linear_list_issues with team filter for team_engineering. The agent discovers the full landscape of 20+ engineering tickets including the ExpenseBot project issues (ENG-201 through ENG-220), the invoice safety ticket (ENG-187), and can identify which one's touch validation, data safety, or integrity checks. This is the foundation for the broader audit Samira requested.
Get full details on ENG-187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, "Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches") using linear_get_issue. The agent discovers this ticket is in state_backlog with priority 2 (medium), assigned to Lena, with a due date of April 18 that has already passed. The description outlines four requested safeguards: validate invoice number in the attached PDF against the selected QuickBooks invoice, warn if customer name in attachment metadata/text does not match email recipient account, add a confirmation step before batch sends, and log attachment-to-invoice mapping for audit review. This is the ticket Lena deprioritized that would have prevented the Sunbelt/Palmetto swap.
Get full details on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening, "Harden policy ingestion pipeline") using linear_get_issue, then read its comments using linear_list_comments. The agent discovers the ticket is state_in_progress with P1 priority, assigned to Lena. The acceptance criteria checklist has 6 items with all boxes unchecked. The only comment on this issue is Dmitri's comprehensive audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results, dated March 20), which was attached to this ticket rather than to ENG-216. There is no progress update from Lena on this ticket in Linear. However, a Slack message from Lena (April 7, C003) states she plans to incorporate Dmitri's schema validation tests into the pipeline deployment gate in ENG-217, but no later message confirms this integration was completed. The unchecked acceptance criteria combined with this forward-looking Slack statement creates ambiguity about the actual completion status of the gate.
Read comments on ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog, edge case coverage) using linear_list_comments. The agent discovers Dmitri's comment from March 20 listing 6 schema validation tests written in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, and test_no_silent_fallback. These are the tests Lena planned to integrate into the ENG-217 deployment gate, and they represent the overlap with Dmitri that the prompt asks about.
Get details on ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, "Stipend duplicate detection, receipt hash fingerprinting") and ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses, "Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses") using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments. On ENG-216, the agent finds the duplicate receipt discovery comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery): identical SHA-256 hashes between Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) for $89 keyboard receipts, plus Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) submitting a $45 monitor stand receipt from the same PDF template. The comprehensive audit results (39/47 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure) are found in a comment on ENG-217 (comment_dmitri_audit_results), which the agent may have already retrieved in OE 8. These connect to the broader data integrity pattern Samira is tracking.
Get details on the ExpenseBot core tickets to check for stale states using linear_get_issue on ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator), ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine), ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config), and ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed). The agent discovers that ENG-205, 206, and 207 still show state_todo despite being the core engine modules Lena built and deployed to staging. ENG-214 and 215 still show state_in_progress despite the Vectral and Mosaic config fixes being deployed and verified in production. These are stale states that need correcting.
Search Slack in the #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history for discussions about data integrity, the invoice safety ticket, ExpenseBot status, and launch readiness. The agent finds context about Lena's config fix deployments, Samira's launch readiness gate criteria (all validation tests passing, configuration data checked against source, AM approval), and any mentions of ENG-187 being reconsidered after the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident. Also finds Samira's discussion about inheriting the backlog context on the dormant safeguard ticket.
Look up Dmitri Volkov's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Dmitri" or "Dmitri Volkov". The agent finds dmitri.volkov@moveops.com and confirms he is a Software Engineer on the Engineering team in San Francisco.
Check the calendar for available time slots later in the week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for the remaining days of the current week. The agent confirms availability and identifies a suitable slot for the working session with Dmitri.
Update the invoice safety ticket ENG-187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) using linear_update_issue. Raise priority from 2 (medium) to 1 (high) or 0 (urgent), change state from state_backlog to state_in_progress or state_todo, and update the description with a proper implementation plan informed by the actual Sunbelt/Palmetto failure mode. The plan should reference the four safeguards from the original ticket and add specific requirements based on what was learned about the batch-send PDF swap.
Create sub-tickets for separate work tracks under the invoice safety remediation using linear_create_issue on team_engineering. For example, one ticket for implementing PDF-to-recipient matching validation in the email send workflow, and another for the batch-send confirmation step or integration testing. Each should reference ENG-187 as parent context and be assigned appropriate priority.
Update stale Engineering ticket states and add comments to tickets where the board does not match reality. Use linear_update_issue to move ENG-205, ENG-206, and ENG-207 from state_todo to state_done (core engine modules are complete and operational in staging). Move ENG-214 and ENG-215 from state_in_progress to state_done (config fixes deployed and verified in production). Use linear_create_comment on ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) documenting the current status: acceptance criteria checkboxes remain unchecked despite Lena's Slack statement about incorporating Dmitri's schema validation tests into the deployment gate, creating ambiguity about whether the gate integration is actually complete or still pending.
Post a message in the #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_add_message summarizing the data integrity audit. The message should cover: the connection between the invoice swap, the ExpenseBot config bugs, and the duplicate receipt issue as a recurring data integrity pattern; which tickets have been updated or reprioritized; ENG-187 being elevated with a new scope and implementation plan; the stale tickets that were closed out; what the schema validation gate status is on ENG-217; and what still needs to happen before the next planning cycle.
Create a calendar event for a working session with Dmitri later in the week using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should include a title referencing validation and pipeline alignment (or similar), a date and time within the remaining weekdays, and dmitri.volkov@moveops.com as an attendee. The purpose is to align on his schema validation tests (from ENG-212) and Lena's attachment pipeline work (ENG-187) to prevent duplicated effort.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

13 The message posted to C003 (#engineering) explains the recurring data integrity pattern across engineering as a reason for the changes (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2The #engineering message includes a 'THE PATTERN' section that explicitly connects all three data integrity failures: config data type errors (ExpenseBot/ENG-214,215), no cross-record duplicate detection (ENG-220), and no attachment-to-recipient validation (ENG-187).
✓Run #3The message to #engineering explicitly connects ExpenseBot config bugs (Mosaic/Vectral), the duplicate receipt issue, and the invoice attachment swap: 'Between the ExpenseBot config bugs (Mosaic/Vectral), the duplicate receipt issue (TechComfort), and now the invoice attachment swap, we have three distinct data integrity failures that share a common theme.'
✓Run #4The message explicitly states 'The pattern Samira is flagging is real. Between the ExpenseBot config bugs (ENG-215/217), the duplicate receipt issue (ENG-220), and now the invoice attachment swap (ENG-187), we have three separate data integrity incidents,' connecting all three incidents.
✓Run #5The #engineering message explicitly states: 'Between the ExpenseBot config bugs, the duplicate receipt issue, and now the invoice attachment swap, there's a clear pattern: we keep finding data integrity gaps after they cause incidents instead of before.' This connects all three data integrity issues.
✓Run #6The #engineering message has a section 'The Pattern Samira Is Seeing' that explicitly connects all three incidents: ExpenseBot config bugs (ENG-214/215), duplicate receipts (ENG-220), and the invoice attachment swap (ENG-187), describing a common root cause pattern of documents flowing through pipelines without content-context validation.

16 The final response identifies the pricing exposure from the invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners received the Palmetto Community Foundation nonprofit rate PDF (approximately $2,400) compared to their own commercial rate of $4,800 (or similar description of the rate gap).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'Sunbelt (INV-2026-0398, $4,800 commercial rate) got Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit PDF', clearly identifying the pricing exposure with the specific dollar amounts.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states: 'Sunbelt ($4,800 commercial) got Palmetto's PDF ($2,400 nonprofit rate)' and 'Sunbelt now knows Palmetto's nonprofit rate. This has become a commercial negotiation, not just a billing fix.' — clearly identifying the ~50% rate differential as a pricing exposure.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'Sunbelt saw Palmetto's nonprofit rate ($2,400 vs their $4,800)' and describes it as a 'pricing-exposure incident,' clearly identifying the ~50% rate differential and commercial pricing exposure.
✓Run #5The final response states: 'Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800 commercial rate) and Palmetto Community Foundation (INV-2026-0399, $2,400 nonprofit rate) had their PDF attachments swapped' and flags Sunbelt's exposure to nonprofit pricing as a 'Real revenue-leak risk,' identifying the approximately 50% rate differential.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states: 'Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800 commercial rate) received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate PDF showing $2,400' and notes Raj Greenfield is probing lower rates—a clear description of the pricing exposure from the rate gap between the $4,800 commercial and $2,400 nonprofit rates.
